html:
Shandong Polymer Bio-Chemicals, 002476.SZ

At about CNY 5.70, Shandong Polymer is a strange small-cap setup: optically expensive on 2025 reported profit, but potentially cheap if 2026 is the year its oilfield-chemical volume reset becomes operating leverage.

The cleanest variant perception is that 2025 was not actually a profit deterioration year. Reported net income fell 44% to CNY 31.3 million, but that compares against a 2024 inflated by investment gains. Core profit improved sharply: 2025扣非净利润 was CNY 25.4 million, up 506%, on revenue of CNY 603 million, up 12%. Q1 2026 then changed the scale of the story: revenue jumped 78% to CNY 227 million, with core profit up 29% to CNY 12.0 million despite margin pressure and working-capital drag. That Q1 revenue is already 38% of full-year 2025 revenue.

The business is not a sexy “bio” platform today. It is mostly oilfield chemicals, especially polyacrylamide and related EOR chemicals sold into China’s oilfield system, plus a smaller high-margin water treatment business. The 2025 report shows the core setup clearly: oilfield chemicals dominate revenue, water treatment carries much better gross margin, and management is trying to push toward higher-end functional products while ordinary PAM remains oversupplied. The company itself describes the industry as one where ordinary products are weak, high-end functional products are tight, and environmental regulation is forcing weaker capacity out.

That is the actual upside case: not “ACN fell, margins magically expand,” but “volume has already stepped up, the company has integrated microbial acrylamide/PAM technology, and mix can shift from ordinary oilfield chemicals toward higher-end oilfield, water-treatment, surfactant, heavy-oil viscosity reducer, fracturing-fluid, and new technical-service products.” If 2026 revenue lands near CNY 850-950 million instead of the old CNY 500-600 million range, even a modest gross-margin recovery toward 18.5-20.0% can produce a very different earnings base.

The oil backdrop helps. With current crude pricing strong, China’s aging field maintenance and domestic energy-security spending should support EOR chemical demand. This is still tender-driven and Sinopec/CNPC can squeeze suppliers, but the procurement backdrop is not the same as a weak oil cycle. The company’s 2025 report explicitly frames domestic oil and gas stable-supply policy as support for core product demand.

The balance sheet gives the thesis time. Total assets were about CNY 1.14 billion, equity about CNY 950 million, and 2025 leverage was low, with an asset-liability ratio around 16%. Q1 cash was still roughly CNY 448 million. That does not make the stock asset-cheap at a CNY 3.5 billion market cap, but it does mean the company is not financially fragile while it absorbs receivables and inventory swings.

The retail context is important. Recent investor questions were not about PAM spreads, they were about falling share price, medical asset injection, and whether the new controller is using the company as a shell. Management denied any current medical asset injection or backdoor listing plan in the May 15 investor activity record. That matters because it means the current speculation premium can fade. But it also tells you where the torque sits: the shareholder base is watching for transition, not for a one-point change in PAM gross margin.

The main risk is that Q1 was low-quality growth. Revenue rose 78%, but costs rose faster, Q1 operating cash flow was negative CNY 55 million, receivables increased, and 2025 gross margin was still only 17.5%, down slightly year over year. If Q2/H1 confirms that the new volume is low-margin tender volume with slow collections, the stock is not cheap. No dividend was proposed for 2025, and the new-control story adds governance complexity rather than automatic value.

The stock works if the next filings show three things: H1 revenue running above CNY 400 million, gross margin stabilizing above 18%, and cash conversion improving after Q1 procurement and receivable pressure. It works very well if higher-end oilfield and water-treatment products start lifting blended margin rather than merely adding volume.

Targets from about CNY 5.70 today

Bear: CNY 3.90, down 32%. Revenue growth proves low quality, 2026 core net income stalls near CNY 35-45 million, no asset transition appears, and the stock loses its small-cap/control-change premium.

Base: CNY 7.40, up 30%. 2026 revenue reaches CNY 850-900 million, core net income moves toward CNY 75-90 million, and the market begins valuing Baomo on 2027 earnings power rather than stale 2025 reported profit.

Bull: CNY 11.20, up 96%. Revenue exceeds CNY 1.0 billion, gross margin moves above 20% on mix and high-end product growth, core net income approaches CNY 140-160 million, and the new-controller transition plus higher-end oilfield/water-treatment story earns a growth multiple.

metadata.company_name: Shandong Polymer Bio-Chemicals Co., Ltd. Class A
